member.name: T:BaseNode
member.summary: <summary> Internal utility class. </summary> <param name="<N>">the n type</param> <param name="<KeyT>">the key t type</param>

member.name: M:BaseNode.#ctor(N,BaseNodeAddress)
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: M:BaseNode.#ctor(BaseNodeAddress,ExecutorService)
member.summary: Constructor.

member.name: P:BaseNode.Key
member.summary: Extract the key list

member.name: M:BaseNode.lock(this)
member.summary: Return the local host ip address

member.name: M:BaseNode.lock(this)_2
member.summary: Get the gRPC channel for this node

member.name: P:BaseNode.ReadmitTime
member.summary: Timestamp of when this node will be considered healthy again

member.name: P:BaseNode.CurrentBackoff
member.summary: The current backoff duration. Uses exponential backoff so think 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, etc until MaxBackoff is hit

member.name: M:BaseNode.lock(this)_3
member.summary: Minimum backoff used by node when receiving a bad gRPC status

member.name: P:BaseNode.MaxBackoff
member.summary: Get the maximum backoff time

member.name: P:BaseNode.Address
member.summary: Address of this node

member.name: P:BaseNode.BadGrpcStatusCount
member.summary: Get the number of times this node has received a bad gRPC status

member.name: M:BaseNode.ChannelFailedToConnect
member.summary: Did we fail to connect?

member.name: M:BaseNode.ChannelFailedToConnectAsync
member.summary: Asynchronously determine if the channel failed to connect.

member.name: M:BaseNode.IsHealthy
member.summary: Determines if this is node is healthy. Healthy means the node has either not received any bad gRPC statuses, or if it has received bad gRPC status then the node backed off for a period of time.

member.name: M:BaseNode.IncreaseBackoff
member.summary: Used when a node has received a bad gRPC status

member.name: M:BaseNode.DecreaseBackoff
member.summary: Used when a node has not received a bad gRPC status. This means on each request that doesn't get a bad gRPC status the current backoff will be lowered. The point of this is to allow a node which has been performing poorly (receiving several bad gRPC status) to become used again once it stops receiving bad gRPC statuses.

member.name: M:BaseNode.GetRemainingTimeForBackoff
member.summary: Get the amount of time the node has to wait until it's healthy again

member.name: M:BaseNode.GetChannelCredentials
member.summary: Create TLS credentials when transport security is enabled

member.name: M:BaseNode.UnhealthyBackoffRemaining
member.summary: Extract the unhealthy backoff time remaining.

member.name: M:BaseNode.Dispose(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Close the current nodes channel
